title: Rendering layers with different perception quality
abstract: In one implementation, a method of displaying image is performed by a device including one or more processors and non-transitory memory. The method includes obtaining gaze information. The method includes obtaining, based on the gaze information, a first resolution function and a second resolution function different than the first resolution function. The method includes rendering a first layer based on first virtual content and the first resolution function. The method includes rendering a second layer based on second virtual content and the second resolution function. The method includes compositing the first layer and the second layer into an image. The method includes displaying, on the display, the image.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: at a device including a display, one or more processors, and non-transitory memory: obtaining gaze information; obtaining, based on the gaze information, a first resolution function and a second resolution function different than the first resolution function; rendering a first layer based on first virtual content and the first resolution function; rendering a second layer based on second virtual content and the second resolution function; compositing the first layer and the second layer into an image; and displaying, on the display, the image. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the second resolution function has a lower maximum than the first resolution function. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the second resolution function has a lower minimum, faster drop-off, or narrower width than the first resolution function. 4. The method of any of claims 1-3, wherein the first resolution function and the second resolution function are obtained with a same formula with different parameters. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, wherein the first layer is a foreground layer and the second layer is a background layer. 6. The method of any of claims 1-5, wherein the first virtual content includes a user interface element of an application. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein at least one of the first resolution function and the second resolution function is based on a type of the application. 8. The method of claim 6 or 7, wherein the second virtual content includes a virtual environment. 9. The method of any of claims 1-8, wherein at least one of the first resolution function and the second resolution function is based on a power consumption. 10. The method of any of claims 1-9, further comprising: rendering a third layer based on the first virtual content and a third resolution function; rendering a fourth layer based on the second virtual content and a fourth resolution function; compositing the third layer and the fourth layer into an additional image; and displaying, on the display, the additional image concurrently with the image. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the third resolution function and the fourth resolution function are a common resolution function. 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the third resolution function and the fourth resolution function are different resolution functions. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein a difference between a maximum of the first resolution function and a maximum of the second resolution function is different than a difference between a maximum of the third resolution function and a maximum of the fourth resolution function. 14. The method of any of claims 1-13, further comprising: rendering a third layer based on third virtual content; extrapolating a fourth layer based on the second layer; compositing the third layer and the fourth layer into an additional image; and displaying, on the display after displaying the image, the additional image. 15. A device comprising: a display; a non-transitory memory; and one or more processors to: obtain gaze information; obtain, based on the gaze information, a first resolution function and a second resolution function different than the first resolution function; render a first layer based on first virtual content and the first resolution function; render a second layer based on second virtual content and the second resolution function; composite the first layer and the second layer into an image; and display, on the display, the image. 16. A device comprising: a display one or more processors; non-transitory memory; and one or more programs stored in the non-transitory memory, which, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the device to perform any of the methods of claims 1-14. 17. A non-transitory memory storing one or more programs, which, when executed by one or more processors of a device including a display, cause the device to perform any of the methods of claims 1-14. 18. A device comprising: a display; one or more processors; a non-transitory memory; and means for causing the device to perform any of the methods of claims 1-14. 19. A method comprising: at a device including a display, one or more processors, and non-transitory memory: rendering a first layer for a first display time based on first virtual content; rendering a second layer for the first display time based on second virtual content; compositing the first layer and the second layer into a first image; displaying, on the display at the first display time, the first image; rendering a third layer for a second display time based on third virtual content; extrapolating a fourth layer for the second display time based on the second layer and a predicted pose of the device at the second display time; compositing the third layer and the fourth layer into a second image; and displaying, on the display at the second display time, the second image. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein extrapolating the fourth layer includes performing a homographic transform. 21. The method of claim 19 or 20, wherein extrapolating the fourth layer includes determining, for a plurality of pixels of the fourth layer, corresponding pixel locations of the second layer. 22. The method of any of claims 19-21, wherein extrapolating at least a portion of the fourth layer is based on a single depth. 23. The method of any of claims 19-22, wherein extrapolating at least a portion of the fourth layer is based on a plurality of depths. 24. The method of any of claims 19-23, wherein the first layer is a foreground layer and the second layer is a background layer. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein the foreground layer includes a user interface element of an application. 26. The method of claim 24 or 25, wherein a rendering frame rate of the background layer is based on a power consumption. 27. The method of any of claims 24-26, wherein a rendering frame rate of the background layer is based on a type of the application. 28. The method of any of claims 24-27, wherein the background layer includes a virtual environment. 29. The method of any of claims 19-28, further comprising: extrapolating a fifth layer for a third display time based on the third layer and a predicted pose of the device at the third display time; extrapolating a sixth layer for the third display time based on the fourth layer and the predicted pose of the device at the third display time; compositing the fifth layer and the sixth layer into a third image; and displaying, on the display at the third display time, the third image. 30. The method of any of claims 19-29, wherein rendering the first layer is based on a first resolution function and rendering the second layer is based on a second resolution function different than the first resolution function. 31. The method of any of claims 19-29, wherein rendering the first layer and rendering the second layer is based on a common resolution function. 32. A device comprising: a display; a non-transitory memory; and one or more processors to: render a first layer for a first display time based on first virtual content; render a second layer for the first display time based on second virtual content; composite the first layer and the second layer into a first image; display, on the display at the first display time, the first image; render a third layer for a second display time based on third virtual content; extrapolate a fourth layer for the second display time based on the second layer and a predicted pose of the device at the second display time; composite the third layer and the fourth layer into a second image; and display, on the display at the second display time, the second image. 33. A device comprising: a display one or more processors; non-transitory memory; and one or more programs stored in the non-transitory memory, which, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the device to perform any of the methods of claims 19-31. 34. A non-transitory memory storing one or more programs, which, when executed by one or more processors of a device including a display, cause the device to perform any of the methods of claims 19-31. 35. A device comprising: a display; one or more processors; a non-transitory memory; and means for causing the device to perform any of the methods of claims 19-31. 36. A method comprising: at a device including a display, one or more processors, and non-transitory memory: loading, into the non-transitory memory, an application; executing, using the one or more processors, the application to display, on the display at a location in a volumetric environment, a virtual object; detecting an unloading trigger; in response to detecting the unloading trigger: storing, in the non-transitory memory, a snapshot of the virtual object; storing, in the non-transitory memory, the location in the volumetric environment; and unloading, from the non-transitory memory, the application; and while the application is unloaded from the non-transitory memory, displaying the snapshot at the location in the volumetric environment. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein detecting the unloading trigger includes determining an amount of available processing power. 38. The method of claim 36 or 37, wherein detecting the unloading trigger includes determining that the virtual object is not displayed. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein determining that the virtual object is not displayed includes determining that the virtual object is outside a field-of-view displayed on the display. 40. The method of claim 38 or 39, wherein determining that the virtual object is not displayed includes determining that the virtual object is occluded by another virtual object. 41. The method of any of claims 36-40, wherein detecting the unloading trigger includes determining a focus of a user. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein determining the focus of the user is based on a gaze of the user. 43. The method of claim 41 or 42, wherein determining the focus of the user is based on user interaction. 44. The method of any of claims 36-43, wherein detecting the unloading trigger includes determining a priority of the application. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein determining the priority of the application is based on an update rate of the application. 46. The method of any of claims 36-45, wherein the virtual object is a two-dimensional virtual object. 47. The method of any of claims 36-45, wherein the virtual object is a three-dimensional virtual object. 48. The method of any of claims 36-47, wherein storing the snapshot of the virtual object includes storing an image of the virtual object from a virtual camera pose in the volumetric environment. 49. The method of claim 48, wherein storing the snapshot of the virtual object includes storing multiple images of the virtual object from multiple virtual camera poses in the volumetric environment. 50. The method of any of claims 36-49, wherein storing the snapshot of the virtual object includes storing a texture map of the virtual object. 51. The method of any of claims 36-50, further comprising: detecting a reloading trigger; and in response to detecting the reloading trigger: reloading, into the non-transitory memory, the application; and executing, using the one or more processors, the application to display, on the display at the location in the volumetric environment, the virtual object. 52. The method of claim 51, further comprising, in response to detecting the reloading trigger, ceasing to display the snapshot at the location in the volumetric environment. 53. A device comprising: a display; a non-transitory memory; and one or more processors to: load, into the non-transitory memory, an application; execute, using the one or more processors, the application to display, on the display at a location in a volumetric environment, a virtual object; detect an unloading trigger; 

 in response to detecting the unloading trigger: store, in the non-transitory memory, a snapshot of the virtual object; store, in the non-transitory memory, the location in the volumetric environment; and unload, from the non-transitory memory, the application; and while the application is unloaded from the non-transitory memory, display the snapshot at the location in the volumetric environment. 54. A device comprising: a display one or more processors; non-transitory memory; and one or more programs stored in the non-transitory memory, which, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the device to perform any of the methods of claims 36-52. 55. A non-transitory memory storing one or more programs, which, when executed by one or more processors of a device including a display, cause the device to perform any of the methods of claims 36-52. 56. A device comprising: a display; one or more processors; a non-transitory memory; and means for causing the device to perform any of the methods of claims 36-52.
